--- title: cogging keywords: fastai sidebar: home_sidebar nb_path: "05_cogging.ipynb" ---
{% raw %}
{% endraw %} {% raw %}
{% endraw %}

pole embrace for minimum cogging torque is calculated as

Ns: number of slots
Np; number of poles
Nc: LCM(Ns, Np)

optimum pole embrace: $j \dfrac{Np}{Nc}$ for $j \in \mathbb{Z}$

{% raw %}

cogging[source]

cogging(s, r)

{% endraw %} {% raw %}
{% endraw %}

usage example

{% raw %} 
Ns = 12            # stator slots
Np = 10            # rotor poles
w_layers = 1       # winding layers
pp = 2             # parallel paths
pitch = 1          # slot pitch
OD = 125           # stator outer diameter
id_f = 0.6         # stator id fraction
bs0_f = 0.2        # slot opening fraction
hs0 = 2            # shoe height
hs1 = 0.1          # shoe slope height
hs2_f = 0.72       # tooth height fraction
tw_f = 0.54         # tooth width as fraction of tooth shoe span
r0 = 0.1           # radius of curvature
len = 60           # stack length
turns = 38         # turns per coil
slot_fill = 0.45    # copper slot fill factor

s1 = stator.Stator(Ns, Np, w_layers, pp, pitch, OD, id_f, bs0_f, hs0, hs1, hs2_f, tw_f, r0, len, turns, slot_fill)
s1.calc_phase_resistance()
print('phase resistance: {0:.4f} Ohms'.format(s1.phase_res))

phase resistance: 0.0180 Ohms
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
OD = 73
Np = 10
g = 0.9
ID = 30
magnet_pitch_f = 0.83
magnet_thickness_f = 2*4/(OD-ID)
r1 = rotor.Rotor(OD, Np, g, ID, magnet_pitch_f, magnet_thickness_f, len)
r1.calc_area()

print('Cross section area of \n core: {0:.2f}\n magnet: {1:.2f}'.format(r1.core_area, r1.magnet_area))

magnet thickness  4.0
Cross section area of 
 core: 2611.45
 magnet: 719.68
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
embrace_list = cogging(s1,r1)
print(embrace_list)

[0.167, 0.333, 0.5, 0.667, 0.833, 1.0]
{% endraw %}
